title: lanneoma triphylla
intro: Lanneoma triphylla, also known as Neem or Indian lilac, is a tropical evergreen tree native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The medicinal properties of Lanneoma triphylla lie in its leaves, bark, and seeds, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, digestive issues, and fevers. The health benefits of Lanneoma triphylla include its antimicrobial, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Lanneoma triphylla can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, capsules, and creams, and is often used in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
